hints.Domini: 'Domini' is a noun in the genitive meaning 'of the Lord'
hints.ergo: 'Ergo' is a conjunction meaning 'therefore' and does not appear here

hints.ablative: Ablative singular of 'ancilla' would be 'ancilla' in form, but context shows this is the subject
hints.vocative: Vocative would be used for direct address; here 'ancilla' is the subject of the declaration

hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension nouns typically end in -us or -um; 'ancilla' ends in -a
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension has varied nominative forms and -is in the genitive
hints.5th declension: 5th declension nouns end in -es; 'ancilla' follows the 1st declension -a pattern

hints.nominative: Nominative would be 'Dominus'; 'Domini' with -i ending is genitive singular
hints.dative: Dative singular would be 'Domino'; 'Domini' is genitive

hints.1st declension: 1st declension nouns typically end in -a; 'Dominus' ends in -us
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension has -is in the genitive; 'Dominus' has -i (Domini)
hints.4th declension: 4th declension -us nouns have -us in the genitive; 'Dominus' has -i (Domini)

translation: Behold the handmaid of the Lord
